LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS CALLAHAN, MCCUNE & WILLIS, PLC, PETER CALLAHAN, LEEH DIBELLO AND ANNA GEHRIGER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE THIRD, SEVENTH AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION. OVERRULED AS THE FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. FOR THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE BREACH OF CONTRACT WHETHER IT BE ORAL, WRITTEN OR IMPLIED IN FACT. (CACI NO. 303. SEE FIRST AM. COMPLAINT, 12 - 16, 74 - 76.) FOR THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE LEGAL AUTHORITY CITED BY DEFENDANTS, BUEHLER V. SHARDELLATI (1985) 170 CAL.APP.3D 1125, 1143 - 1143 DOES NOT STAND FOR THE PROPOSITION ASSERTED. (POINTS & AUTH., 5:1-3.) THE BUEHLER COURT DID NOT FIND THAT A BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY CLAIM MUST BE DISMISSED AS REDUNDANT IF IT BASED ON THE SAME FACTS AS A NEGLIGENCE CLAIM. FOR THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANTS DEMUR ONLY TO THE NEGLIGENCE PER SE ALLEGATIONS, AND NOT THE NEGLIGENCE ALLEGATIONS. A DEMURRER DOES NOT LIE AS TO PART OF A CAUSE OF ACTION. FOR THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE EXTREME, AND OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT. FOR THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS WITH THE REQUISITE SPECIFICITY TO ALLEGE FRAUD. THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE THIRD, SIXTH AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NOT UNCERTAIN. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT SUBMITTED ON FEB-23-2015 BY ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH. MATTER TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ONLY AS TO THE COURT'S RULING ON THE DEMURRER AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF ATTORNEY-CLIENT CONTRACT, AND THE TENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION. THE COURT ADOPTS THE FOLLOWING PORTIONS OF ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE TO PERMIT PLAINTIFFS TO ALLEGE, IF THEY CAN, CONDUCT AMOUNTING TO GENERAL, AS OPPOSED TO PROFESSIONAL, NEGLIGENCE (PLAINTIFFS HAVE REQUESTED LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THIS CAUSE OF ACTION WHICH THE COURT GRANTS). THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION. THIS CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT PLED WITH THE REQUISITE SPECIFICITY, AND THE DEFECT WAS NOT CURED AFTER THE COURT GAVE LEAVE TO AMEND THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BOTH EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIMS BECAUSE THERE ARE NO ALLEGATIONS OF OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT AND THERE IS NO BASIS FOR A NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIM. FINALLY, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE IMPLIED EQUITABLE INDEMNITY CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE IT FAILS TO ALLEGE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO STATE SUCH A CAUSE OF ACTION, AND THE OPPOSITION FAILS TO OFFER ANY CURE. THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY, AND RULES THAT THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS THAT CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT WILL PREPARE AN ORDER AFTER THE COURT RULES ON THE MATTERS THAT HAVE BEEN TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR #11185; DEPT. 302. Defendant Anna Gehriger'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Prior Court Orders, And For Additional Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jonathan Bornstein, Esq. (415-235-8231) of J.H. Bornstein for Plaintiff.
Michael Mcconathy, Esq. (415-397-2800) of Lynch Gillardi & Grummer for Defendant.
Alexander Drury, Esq. (415-646-4704) of Maynard Cooper & Gale for Defendant Hartford.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces written discovery is at issue. Plaintiff reports he did not receive any papers. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Anna Gehriger'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Prior Court Orders, And For Additional Sanctions. Defendant Anna Gehriger's request for terminating sanctions is granted. All of the claims of plaintiffs Hubert Wong and Judith Wong alleged against Ms. Gehriger are dismissed. The papers filed by Ms. Gerhiger on June 23, 2017 establish that plaintiffs and their counsel have continued to ignore their discovery obligations to the detriment of defendants. Despite being given the opportunity to do so, plaintiffs have not filed any papers contesting any of the discovery violations asserted in Ms. Gehriger's June 23, 2017 papers. Plaintiffs' lack of any response to the June 23, 2017 papers is a concession of the accuracy of those papers. This court has gone to great lengths to avoid issuing terminating sanctions, but each time the court's indulgence and admonition to plaintiffs' counsel has been met with further discovery non-compliance. The egregious discovery abuse committed by plaintiffs and their counsel over an extended period of time and the resulting prejudice and huge cost to defendants warrants the extreme measure of ending this case in favor of Ms. Gehriger. Order is signed. Party is welcome to bring a motion for reconsideration. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)

Off Calendar Mar-05-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JONATHAN BORNSTEIN of the Law Offices of Bornstein & Bornstein, Tel No. (415) 409-7611 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. GARY ITTIG of Lynch, Gilari & Grummer, APC Tel No. (415) 397-2800 appearing for the Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court takes DEFENDANT CALLAHAN, MCCUNE & WILLIS, PLC, PETER CALLAHAN, LEEH DIBELLO, ANNA GEHRIGER'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT under submission. Matter taken under submission only as to the Court's ruling on the Demurrer as to the third cause of action for breach of attorney-client contract, and the tenth cause of action for negligent misrepresentation. The Court will be adopting the following portions of its tentative ruling: The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the cause of action for negligence to permit Plaintiffs to allege, if they can, conduct amounting to general, as opposed to professional, negligence (Plaintiffs have requested leave to amend as to this cause of action which the Court grants). The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the fraud cause of action. This cause of action is not pled with the requisite specificity, and the defect was not cured after the court gave leave to amend the first amended complaint. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to both emotional distress claims because there are no allegations of outrageous conduct and there is no basis for a negligent infliction of emotional distress claim. Finally, the Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the implied equitable indemnity cause of action because it fails to allege facts sufficient to state such a cause of action, and the opposition fails to offer any cure. The Court will be vacating its tentative ruling as to the fifth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, and will rule that the Demurrer is overruled as that cause of action. The Court will prepare an order after the Court rules on the matters that have been taken under submission. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 (302/EHG)

For the third cause of action, Plaintiffs have not pled sufficient facts to allege breach of contract whether it be oral, written or implied in fact. (CACI no. 303. See First Am. Complaint, 12 - 16, 74 - 76.) For the fifth cause of action, the legal authority cited by Defendants, Buehler v. Shardellati (1985) 170 Cal.App.3d 1125, 1143 - 1143 does not stand for the proposition asserted. (Points & Auth., 5:1-3.) The Buehler Court did not find that a breach of fiduciary duty claim must be dismissed as redundant if it based on the same facts as a negligence claim. For the sixth cause of action, Defendants demur only to the negligence per se allegations, and not the negligence allegations. A demurrer does not lie as to part of a cause of action. For the seventh cause of action, Plaintiffs have not pled sufficient facts to allege extreme, and outrageous conduct. For the ninth cause of action, Plaintiffs have not pled sufficient facts with the requisite specificity to allege fraud. The allegations of the third, sixth and ninth causes of action are not uncertain. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
